title：Response to your recent email "A Joint Message to Students from Heads of Universities / Institutions"

Dear Sir/Madam.

I recently received your Joint Message to Students. I would like to firstly express gratitude to your concern on current social issues.

As far as I can understand, you only wrote "We are all very shocked and deeply saddened by the incidents in Hong Kong last weekend. We also note that further activities are being planned in Yuen Long this coming weekend." regarding to the terror attack in Yuen Long. You express no consolation--not even simple sympathy--to the victims of such horrific incident both injured and horrified. Neither did you condemn the terrorists in white, nor did you urge the government to handle this case in due course. Perhaps I have gone too far to ask you to question the role of the Police in such attack.

You also wrote that "As the situation is still evolving and can become extremely volatile and dangerous, we want to make the strongest plea to you to stay away for your personal safety. Please take good care of yourselves." I understand that personal safety should be concerned at all times. However I do not see such compromise on our civil rights for personal safety is best for the society. Your attitude on such attacks grants success on the spread of white terror.

In the darkest time during which your students live under the constant fear for their own safety regardless of their participation in any social movement--one that I might remind you is nothing but an exercise of our civil rights and duty--we do wish to seek comfort from the very institutions that have educated us what it means to seek knowledge and perform critical thinking. Yet, I cannot help but be utterly disappointed that our institution's administration seems not to be willing to provide such comfort--unlike our professors who have been by our side from the very beginning.

I ask not of your direct aid or support, but merely your sympathy and care for your own students, because at the end of the day, what is an academic institution without its healthy and free-thinking students? I urge you to leave your strongest plea to our Police Department to keep perform their duty as civil servants and keep our society safe and in order through different means to guarantee no attacks could be done on recent peaceful protests. I also make the strongest plea to you Heads of Universities and Institutions to encourage students express their political views instead of giving in to triads, the Police force and the government.

Again, I am pleased that you express concern on current social movements. Thanks but no thanks.

（二選一）Concerned graduate／ current student

八大訴求
一、徹底撤回修例
二、收回暴動定義
三、撤銷對今為所有反送中抗爭者控罪
四、徹底追究警隊濫權情況
五、以行政命令解散立法會，立即實行雙真普選
六、要求永不落實「社會信用評分系統」
七、要求移除全港「多功能智慧燈柱」
八、追究元朗鄉黑暴徒及警察失蹤
